title: Freelancer tax guide: How to report overseas client income and pay taxes in India
summary: Freelancers in India earning from foreign clients must report this income on their tax returns, as it is fully taxable. They can opt for presumptive taxation under Section 44ADA if conditions are met. Details here.&nbsp;

title: Using credit card to buy property abroad? Here’s why you may attract ED scrutiny
summary: Individuals making overseas property payments via international credit cards should be aware of FEMA regulations. Such transactions may invite scrutiny, especially if not routed through authorised channels. Details here.

title: Life Insurance Corporation: Two New Jeevan Sathi plans launch from June — Check eligibility, policy features, benefits

title: Can recovery agents threaten or harass you? Here's what the new RBI rules say – ‘no excessive calls’
summary: RBI’s 2026 recovery rules aim to curb harassment and strengthen borrower protection, with strict action against intimidation by banks and NBFC agents. Recovery agents cannot harass borrowers, while borrowers must also understand and fulfil their financial responsibilities.

title: How to avoid TDS deduction on EPF withdrawal: Eligibility and rules explained
summary: Withdrawing from your EPF before five years may incur TDS if the amount is over ₹50,000. Exemptions exist for those with nil tax liability who can submit Form 121. Details here.&nbsp;

title: 20/4/10 rule explained: How this budgeting formula can prevent car loan stress
summary: Hooked on a shiny car but worried about loan stress? Start with the 20/4/10 rule to protect your finances: 20% down payment, 4-year loan, and total car costs under 10% of your monthly income. Read on to understand the basic concepts before you lock in with a fresh car loan EMI.&nbsp;
